title: Music Motif
wikitext:
[]: Music Motif             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Thu Jul 22, 2010 8:16 pm So far in 1.0 and 2.0, there have been very climatic, heavy bass, heavy choral songs used near the end of each movie. These songs for 1.0 and 2.0 are "Angels of Doom" and "The Final Decision We All Must Take," respectively. These two songs are VERY similar in tone and sound, and are very representative of the actions in the film. Angels of Doom: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=o40Hhgs_Mlk The Final Decision: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kQBi2IIMmpQ&feature=related So, will this continue in "Q" and "Final?"

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 22, 2010 10:12 pm Yes.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Jul 23, 2010 3:46 am That's just Sagisu's style for bombastic music. Check out this piece from the Bleach soundtrack, also composed by Sagisu. It's basically interchangeable with any of the "epic" tracks from the NME soundtracks. I wouldn't mind if they cut back on this type of music for the following films. All these howling choruses and heavy handed strings can punctuate epic moments nicely, but honestly, they get boring pretty fast. Maybe even introduce a few pieces composed by someone other (lolYoko Kanno i said it now i'm cool) than Sagisu featured in prominent moments, and i don't necessarily mean classical composers or retro music. Just something to liven the sound a bit.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Fri Jul 23, 2010 3:49 am SaltyJoe wrote: That's just Sagisu's style for bombastic music. Check out this piece from the Bleach soundtrack, also composed by Sagisu. It's basically interchangeable with any of the "epic" tracks from the NME soundtracks. I wouldn't mind if they cut back on this type of music for the following films. All these howling choruses and heavy handed strings can punctuate epic moments nicely, but honestly, they get boring pretty fast. Maybe even introduce a few pieces composed by someone other (lolYoko Kanno i said it now i'm cool) than Sagisu featured in prominent moments, and i don't necessarily mean classical composers or retro music. Just something to liven the sound a bit.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Jul 23, 2010 4:07 am I was just being humorous, or so i thought. When "anime" and "soundtrack" are mentioned in the same context, "Yoko Kanno" is one of the first associations. Someone who i would actually like to get involved with an Evangelion related OST is Tsutomu Ohashi who composed the soundtrack for the "Akira" feature film (under the pseudonym Shoji Yamashiro), but as far as i know he hasn't done any composing since 1988, so i guess that's just a dream.

[]:             Quote Post by edmundasBakashinji » Fri Jul 23, 2010 7:37 am better hope Shiro-san will find some better version of some classical music recordings.... yet I love Shiro-san's music is because he wisely use many renown pieces in classical music field...and it enlightens me the importance of classical music...motivates me to keep study in Music theories, even my piano... I've found some books said Bach and Beethoven are respectively the The Old Testament & The New Testament of Music...very relevant for religious anime as Evangelion.... Gonna be disappointed if Symphony No.9 won't appear again in Q...

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Fri Jul 23, 2010 8:14 am I hope so, but I'm also hoping for some of the insanity of Mother is the first Other. Any arrangement of that song is godly. Not sure what Salty is talking about when he says these songs become boring quickly. I think they each had their own personality that made them interesting each time.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Jul 23, 2010 10:23 am BeoX2 wrote: Bebop and Eva go together like grapefruit juice and cake. Ugh.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jul 23, 2010 11:11 am The problem is not Sagisu - he did the original series as well - but the tone of the pieces he's using. This, while quite in keeping with the tone of Rebuild in general, doesn't feel any more like NGE than the rest of Rebuild does. I'd expect to hear "Fate" or "In My Spirit" in a Michael Bay movie, not in Evangelion. Incidentally I find it rather depressing that while Rebuild appears to be going in the Rei shipping it has yet to actually use any of the Rei themes. If memory serves.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jul 23, 2010 12:36 pm Rei's themes are in 1.0, and can be heard on both 1.0 OSTs. Rei Opus IV = A-14 (I forget the title but S2 Works has two sets of A-13 or -14 pieces.) Rei Opus V = A-1 Rei I.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Jul 23, 2010 12:43 pm ^ I'm dead certain that the Rei piano theme was in 1.0. EDIT: Ninja posted. Clover wrote: Not sure what Salty is talking about when he says these songs become boring quickly

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Fri Jul 23, 2010 1:03 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Have you not seen the EvaBebop mashup OP then?

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sat Jul 24, 2010 7:44 pm SaltyJoe wrote: ^ I'm dead certain that the Rei piano theme was in 1.0. EDIT: Ninja posted. The new breed of EPIC tracks introduced in Rebuild, especially 2.0. In another thread i already expressed a desire to see (hear) certain oldies like "Borderline Case" to make a comeback. And of course, the more gratious use of classical music with pretentious cinematography in the future films, the better. Anyways, though i'm sure that there will be more ragechorus-type pieces in the following films, since there are less angels to go through, i guess they won't be so stacked upon each other.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sun Jul 25, 2010 2:08 am Warren Peace wrote: I'm not sure what you'd put in place of "Final Decision" and "Angel of Doom" that isn't EPIC

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Jul 25, 2010 5:53 am -

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Jul 25, 2010 12:40 pm SaltyJoe wrote: Someone who i would actually like to get involved with an Evangelion related OST is Tsutomu Ohashi who composed the soundtrack for the "Akira" feature film (under the pseudonym Shoji Yamashiro)

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sun Jul 25, 2010 11:40 pm SaltyJoe wrote: They mash with their respective scenes very well, yes. But those aren't pieces i would listen to on their own.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sun Jul 25, 2010 11:44 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Have you not seen the EvaBebop mashup OP then?

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Mon Jul 26, 2010 7:35 am Warren Peace wrote: should cinema music be required to stand on its own?

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Jul 27, 2010 3:21 am I wouldn't mind them tacking whatever they want onto it, as long as it doesn't detract from the score. I really liked 2.0's design, with the "movie edit" and "full edit" discs. Some of the peices were shortened too dramatically, and I'm glad to hear the whole thing (Les Betes!). If they use FMTTM in Rebuild, I hope it's Utada's. That's the best Eva version of the song yet.
